pages.full_text:
Saturday June 12th

View from table + chairs behind hedge at camp site Eastward, Shropshire.
we need to buy, bread milk tea glue cafetiere

    We went to Stokesay Castle + each listened to a tape recording of its history as we wandered around. A beautiful place. It surrendered to Cromwell's troops in the civil war. Lawrence of Ludlow had permission to build a castle from the king. It wasn't one of the Marches Castles.
Enroute to the Elan Valley Reservoirs we had an outdoor lunch at a pub. There were 3 or more Reservoirs, built 1886-19[illegible]. At any time they had 5,000 men working on them, 12,000 men were on the books. A final reservoir was opened by Queen Elizabeth, her first appointment on ascending
pages.route_sketch_description: Pencil sketch at top of page showing a landscape view of rolling hills/mountains, with some tree/vegetation outlines, labelled as the view from the campsite at Eastward, Shropshire.
pages.notes: Ink is green for main diary text; pencil for the landscape sketch. The date 'Saturday June 12th' fell on a Saturday in 2004 and 2010 within the volume's range (2003-2019); mid-volume position (page 174 of 373) loosely suggests 2010 is more likely, but year cannot be confirmed from page content alone. The reservoir construction date reads '1886-19' followed by partially legible digits that may be '03' or '08' — transcribed as [illegible]. 'Eastward' could also read 'Eastwood' — best reading given context of Shropshire campsite is 'Eastward'.

Page contains a single glued-in colour photograph of Stokesay Castle showing the half-timbered gatehouse and ruined curtain walls with a graveyard in the foreground. The caption below the photo is handwritten in green ink.
